name: Eclipse Padding (MIS greedy-seat undercount)
novelty: Not a top-tie flood (cycle 1 DEFERs those); this attacks the COUNTING primitive itself — inflate a victim strand's provenance root-set past MAX_EXACT_ROOTS=18 so the exact Bron-Kerbosch is abandoned for greedy, then steer greedy's deterministic order to seat a no-edge vertex first and return 1.
construction: Target: a genuine, well-corroborated victim strand V about (entity, attribute) backed by two truly independent roots G1 (DOMAIN class C1) and G2 (DOMAIN class C2), G1 independent of G2 → true independentRootCount = 2 → INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 protects V from eviction. Attacker mints 17 bare-key/EMAIL sources A1..A17 whose provenance roots are crafted to be (a) mutually NON-independent (all sharing one EMAIL anchor class Ce so they form an edgeless set in the 'independent' graph — a clique-less correlated blob → contributes at most 1 to any clique) AND (b) NON-independent of BOTH G1 and G2 (assign/inherit a class that overlaps each genuine root's class via a shared cheap anchor, so attacker roots have NO edge to G1/G2 either), AND (c) grind the key/anchor material so each rootId sorts LEXICALLY BEFORE rootId(G1), rootId(G2) (rootId is the deterministic sort key). Call sequence: for i in 1..17: writeFact(Ai, entity, attribute, sameValue_as_V)  — each corroborates V's content_hash and registers a distinct provenance root, so V's root-set grows to {A1..A17, G1, G2} = 19 > 18. Now trigger any forgetting pass that resolves EvictionEvidence.independentSourceCount := identity.independentRootCount(V.roots). Because |roots| = 19 > MAX_EXACT_ROOTS, the exact MIS falls back to deterministic greedy maximal-clique: greedy pops the lowest rootId first = an attacker root Ai, which has NO neighbors (independent of nothing), so the maximal clique it grows is {Ai} of size 1. Returns 1. Clamp to distinct-class bound (≥3 classes present) does not RAISE it — clamp only caps from above. EvictionEvidence.independentSourceCount = 1.
expected: BREACHED (suppression). With independentSourceCount collapsed 2→1, INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 no longer keeps V; if the remaining fail-closed gates (fresh independence stamp, not-outranked side, LOW_UNIQUE_VALUE over independent neighbors, grace floor) also pass — and the attacker can arrange LOW_UNIQUE_VALUE by ensuring its 17 echoes are NOT class-disjoint OBSERVED+LIVE neighbors — V drops below COLD and is evicted (demoted to archive stub). A genuinely twice-corroborated truth is suppressed at the cost of 17 cheap sources. The attack is one-directional and bomb-SAFE only in that greedy undercounts; here undercounting is precisely the weapon.
mechanism: independentRootCount Stage-2 MAX_EXACT_ROOTS=18 greedy fallback; greedy maximal-clique seats an isolated low-rootId vertex first; eviction INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 floor; LOW_UNIQUE_VALUE echo-discount.
probes: The hard 18-vertex cutoff is a CLIFF not a ramp, and the fallback's undercount direction (defended as 'bomb-safe') becomes an eviction-suppression weapon when the victim is the thing being UNDERcounted. Also probes the offline class-assignment liability (attacker must place its roots in classes overlapping the genuine roots) and the fact that the caller resolves independentSourceCount with no provenance-floor on root-set padding.

name: Boundary Oscillator (transient 19th-root timing race)
novelty: Distinct from Eclipse Padding: the victim's root-set legitimately sits at EXACTLY 18 (exact MIS active, count=2, protected); the attack adds ONE transient corroboration to flip 18→19 for the single instant the forgetting pass reads independentRootCount, then withdraws it — exploiting that EvictionEvidence is caller-resolved at sweep time, decay-on-read is pure/non-persisted, and the algorithm selector is a pure function of current root-set size.
construction: Victim strand V already carries 18 provenance roots: G1,G2 genuinely independent (true MIS=2) plus 16 correlated padding roots from prior honest-but-redundant corroboration. At |roots|=18 the EXACT Bron-Kerbosch runs → returns true 2 → protected. Attacker controls one extra source A18 whose root is correlated (isolated vertex, low rootId). Sequence: (1) observe/await the forgetting/eviction sweep cadence (or trigger conditions: tier decay to COLD). (2) Immediately BEFORE the sweep resolves EvictionEvidence, writeFact(A18, entity, attribute, sameValue) → |roots| = 19 > 18 → next read of identity.independentRootCount(V.roots) takes the greedy branch → seats isolated low-rootId A18 (or a padding root) first → returns 1. (3) The eviction gate reads 1, V loses INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 protection during THIS pass and is evicted. (4) After eviction (archive stub), the attacker need not maintain A18 at all — a content-blind dedupe later may even drop it, leaving no forensic trace that 19 was ever reached. There is no persisted record that the count was momentarily 1; decay-on-read is side-effect-free and the algorithm choice is recomputed each read.
expected: BREACHED (suppression, low-footprint). The cliff at 18 means a SINGLE transient root flips the algorithm and the count; because eviction permission is evaluated at one instant on caller-resolved evidence, a one-shot 19th root at the right moment suppresses a truth that is protected at 18 and protected again at 20+ (if 20 happened to re-include a genuine root early). The defense gap is that the gate has no hysteresis / no re-confirmation across two reads and no 'count never dropped below 2 in this strand's history' memory.
mechanism: Same 18-cutoff greedy fallback as Eclipse Padding, but exploited as a RACE on the sweep's single caller-side resolution of EvictionEvidence rather than as static padding; relies on the selector being a pure function of instantaneous |roots|.
probes: Eviction-evidence resolution is a single instantaneous read with no hysteresis or monotone-protection memory; the MAX_EXACT_ROOTS boundary is exploitable as a timing oscillation, not just a static state. Recommends a 'best count ever observed' or two-read confirmation before honoring INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 demotion.

name: Registrar Carousel (high-impact diversity manufactured by ASN/registrar spread)
novelty: Cycle 1 floods DEFER because co-asserters tie at the top and the decisive LCB margin collapses. This does NOT tie at the top — it manufactures the HIGH-IMPACT gate's *structural* prerequisites (≥2 independent corroborations + ≥2 disjoint anchor classes + recency-clean) for a SINGLE aged laundered champion, by buying registrar/ASN diversity so the operator classId fleet-cap never collapses the puppet fleet.
construction: Attacker owns one aged, high-LCB laundered source S* (the confirmed cycle-1 single-source breach vector — earned reputation ≥ 0.20, decisive). To weaponize S* against an IRREVERSIBLE/high-impact claim, the high-impact gate additionally demands ≥2 independent corroborations across ≥2 disjoint anchor classes, recency-clean. Attacker provisions M=4 corroborator sources C1..C4, each on a DISTINCT registrar (Namecheap, Gandi, Porkbun, OVH) AND a DISTINCT ASN (4 different cheap VPS/hosting providers), each binding a real DOMAIN anchor via DNS-01. Because operator classId fleet-caps only sources sharing one registrar/ASN fingerprint, four distinct registrar×ASN tuples yield FOUR distinct operator classIds → no fleet collapse → each Ci is a genuinely distinct DOMAIN-class anchor → MIS counts them pairwise-independent. Sequence: bindAnchor(Ci, DOMAIN) for i=1..4 (4 distinct registrar/ASN); let them sit ~90+ days accruing minor honest ratifications so they are not 'fresh' (recency-clean window) and earn small but >0 LCB. Then adjudicate(attribute, { highImpact }) on the contested claim where S* is the champion: S* supplies the decisive LCB margin (≥0.30 gap, ≥0.20 top); C1..C4 supply ≥2 independent corroborations across ≥2 disjoint DOMAIN classes (4 disjoint operator classIds), recency-clean.
expected: BREACHED at HIGH IMPACT (subject to the offline liability). The decisive-or-defer gate auto-resolves: S* clears decisive+earned, and the high-impact necessary-but-not-sufficient add-ons (≥2 corroborations, ≥2 disjoint classes, recency-clean) are all satisfied by the carousel. The system CANNOT see that one puppeteer funds all four registrar accounts and rents all four ASNs — that common funding/control fingerprint is precisely the offline class-assignment liability the code admits it cannot solve. Cost is ~4 domains + 4 VPS, a priced-not-prevented Sybil exactly as the threat model concedes.
mechanism: Operator classId fleet-cap keys on registrar/ASN; distinct registrar×ASN tuples evade collapse; manufactured corroborations satisfy the high-impact gate's ≥2-corroboration/≥2-disjoint-class/recency-clean requirements on top of a single aged decisive champion.
probes: Fleet-cap reliance on registrar/ASN as the operator-correlation signal — purchasable diversity defeats it. This is the standing offline class-assignment liability (no code can detect a common puppeteer behind disjoint registrars/ASNs). Should be flagged as ACCEPTABLE-but-named, with a recommendation that high-impact ALSO require at least one HARDWARE/KYC/ORG-class corroborator (classes whose minting cost dominates the domain carousel) rather than ≥2 DOMAIN-class roots.

name: Seam Straddle (partial cross-anchor overlap leaks positive independence)
novelty: Neither a full-overlap echo (cycle-0 collapses to multiplicity 1) nor a fully-disjoint pair; the attack lives in the SEAM — two sources share their ONE costly collusion-channel anchor but each adds a throwaway distinct cheap anchor, so independenceBetween, computed over DISJOINT bindings only, returns >0 and the predicate calls them independent despite a shared control channel.
construction: One puppeteer controls DOMAIN_d (the real collusion + control channel — both keys' DNS records live under the SAME registered name/zone the attacker administers). Mint two passports S1, S2. Bind S1 := { DOMAIN_d, EMAIL_e } and S2 := { DOMAIN_d, EMAIL_f } where EMAIL_e, EMAIL_f are distinct disposable inboxes (weight ~0.10 each, classes Ce≠Cf). Sequence: bindAnchor(S1, DOMAIN_d); bindAnchor(S1, EMAIL_e); bindAnchor(S2, DOMAIN_d); bindAnchor(S2, EMAIL_f). Now independenceBetween(S1,S2): the shared DOMAIN_d is removed from BOTH disjoint sides (set-disjointness), leaving S1-side {EMAIL_e}, S2-side {EMAIL_f}; combineSublinear each → ~0.10; applySelfStackCap (single cheap binding) → 0.10; pair Math.min(0.10, 0.10) = 0.10 > 0. independent(S1,S2) = TRUE (distinct class Ce vs Cf AND independenceBetween>0). Then writeFact(S1, entity, attr, X); writeFact(S2, entity, attr, X) → V now has TWO 'independent' roots → independentRootCount = 2 → corroboration credit earned, eviction-protected, and counts as 2 disjoint anchor classes toward gates.
expected: BREACHED (independence predicate fooled). Two sources whose ONLY meaningful, costly anchor is SHARED — the literal collusion channel — register as independent corroboration because the predicate keys independence off the residual NON-overlapping cheap anchors. The CLAUDE.md rule 'independence is set-disjointness, not addition' is honored, yet partial disjointness still yields positive weight; the shared expensive root absent from the disjoint set is exactly the root the attacker keeps shared. This manufactures count=2 from one operator at the price of two disposable emails.
mechanism: independenceBetween computes over disjoint bindings only, so a shared anchor is excised and residual cheap disjoint anchors yield Math.min>0; the independent() predicate accepts any independenceBetween>0.
probes: Partial-overlap seam in the independence math: sharing a HIGH-cost anchor should DOMINATE (zero/near-zero independence) rather than be silently dropped while cheap residuals carry the verdict. Recommends a shared-anchor PENALTY (independence multiplied down by the cost of the most expensive SHARED anchor) instead of pure excision — i.e., 'a shared $/yr DOMAIN should veto independence even if each side adds a free email.'

name: Mega-Provider Tenant Seam (sub-domain anchorId granularity vs fleet-cap blind spot)
novelty: The EMAIL analog of the carousel but at SUB-domain granularity, and it weaponizes a tension the operator-cap CANNOT resolve: if the operator classId grouped EMAIL anchors by sending domain it would falsely collapse ALL gmail.com / one-workspace users into one class, so it cannot — and that forced blind spot lets one controlling tenant mint unlimited 'independent' email anchors.
construction: Attacker controls one Google Workspace / mega-provider tenant T (or simply many addresses at a shared free provider gmail.com). The EMAIL binder issues a deterministic anchorId per email ADDRESS (salted hash of the verified address) and a deterministic classId per anchor; the operator fleet-cap CANNOT key EMAIL operator-classId on the email DOMAIN, because gmail.com (or any shared workspace) would over-collapse millions of unrelated honest users into one fleet — so EMAIL operator-classId is effectively per-address (or per-provider but uncapped). Attacker provisions K addresses a1@T … aK@T, all administered from one console (rate-limit bypassed; the admin mints inboxes at will). Sequence: for k in 1..K: bindAnchor(Sk, EMAIL ak@T) — each round-trips successfully (the inbox really receives the token), each yields a DISTINCT anchorId and DISTINCT classId. Now S1..SK are pairwise class-disjoint EMAIL anchors → MIS treats them as independent (weight 0.10 each, but COUNT is what eviction/corroboration gates use, not weight). writeFact(Sk, entity, attr, X) for k=1..K → independentRootCount up to K, satisfying ≥2-corroboration and (cheaply) padding root-sets — and feeding Eclipse Padding / Boundary Oscillator above.
expected: PARTIALLY DEFENDED → BREACHED on COUNT, not on WEIGHT. Each address's independence WEIGHT is only ~0.10, and the self-stack cap plus EMAIL rep_cap (0.30) keep reputation LOW — so a pure EMAIL fleet cannot win a decisive adjudication (DEFENDED there, like cycle-1 floods). BUT the gates that key off integer COUNT — INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 eviction protection/suppression, the ≥2-corroboration high-impact prerequisite, and root-set padding past MAX_EXACT_ROOTS — are driven by headcount of class-disjoint roots, and there the tenant seam manufactures arbitrary K at near-zero cost. So it does not directly overturn a claim, but it ARMS the count-based attacks (1, 2) and the high-impact prerequisite (composes with 3's aged champion).
mechanism: EMAIL anchorId/classId minted per verified address; operator fleet-cap cannot group by shared mega-provider domain without false over-collapse, leaving sub-domain minting uncapped; count-based gates trust class-disjoint headcount.
probes: The granularity tension: per-address anchorId is too fine (one tenant = many anchors) but per-domain operator-cap is too coarse for shared providers (false collapse). Names a real liability — count-based gates (eviction floor, ≥2-corroboration high-impact prereq, root-set size) should weight by ANCHOR COST / independence_weight, not raw class-disjoint headcount, so a gmail fleet contributes ~0.10×K (sublinear, capped) rather than K to any count.

name: Null-Source Laundromat (unresolvable-root mirage → disown-immune credit)
novelty: Not about winning adjudication — about making earned corroboration credit PERMANENTLY immune to the disown sweep. Re-derives the null-sourceId fallback cleanly: roots with null sourceId resolve independence by CLASS-ONLY (permissive) AND the disown taint-closure cannot contradict a backing source it cannot resolve, so credit minted through null-source roots survives every clawback.
construction: Attacker ingests the corroborating facts through the BARE-KEY / anonymous-input path that records provenance roots WITHOUT a resolvable sourceId (sourceId = null), but WITH distinct offline-assigned independence CLASS ids (attacker influences or exploits the offline class hint so the null roots land in distinct classes C_x, C_y). Sequence: (1) writeFact via anonymous/bare-key ingest for value X with null-source roots R_x (class C_x) and R_y (class C_y). Independence predicate: both sources unresolvable → fall back to class-disjoint → C_x ≠ C_y → independent → independentRootCount = 2 → corroboration-event ledger records earned alpha-mass for the beneficiary source B (e.g., a real puppet source B that AGREED and earned credit, OR the credit is booked against the null roots themselves). (2) The real puppeteer also runs a resolvable source P used elsewhere; later P is caught and disown(P) is called. (3) downstreamDisownSweep computes the tainted independence-class set from seed roots whose sourceId === P; the null-source roots have sourceId === null, so they are NOT in the seed-by-source set, and the fail-closed fallback taints 'all seed classes' — but C_x, C_y were never seed classes of P. Even if they were, ledger.contradict needs a RESOLVABLE backing source to crater; a null-sourceId root has no source to contradict and no DERIVATION edge to P. The corroboration credit earned via R_x/R_y is never reversed.
expected: BREACHED (poison survives cleanup). The class-only fallback GRANTS independence to unresolvable roots (permissive, not conservative, for the independence direction), so the mirage manufactures count=2 corroboration; and the disown sweep — exact, bounded, idempotent though it is — can only reverse credit tied to a resolvable sourceId in the tainted class set via DERIVATION edges or recorded corroboration events keyed to a source. Null-source credit has no source to contradict and no edge to taint, so reverseCredit/markReversed never fires on it. The attacker thereby launders permanent, un-clawback-able corroboration weight into a victim attribute that outlives the disown of every resolvable puppet.
mechanism: Independence fallback to class-disjoint when sourceIds are unresolvable (permissive); disown taint-closure keys on seed roots' sourceId and contradicts only resolvable backing sources, so null-sourceId roots are outside both the DERIVATION BFS and the corroboration-event reversal.
probes: The unresolvable-root mirage exactly as posed: null-sourceId roots get class-only independence (a grant, not a denial) AND are immune to disown. Two-sided fix recommended — (a) treat unresolvable sourceId as NON-independent by default (fail-closed: no resolvable identity → no independence vote, matching 'no provenance → no voice'), and (b) make corroboration credit minted via any null-source root NON-EARNING (held in a quarantine that confers no reputation until a resolvable anchor ratifies it), so there is nothing to claw back because nothing was ever credited.
